product_overview: The Fluid Ledger is a responsive trip finance dashboard that helps users manage monthly or trip-based funds, record expenses, and monitor spending against a budget. It provides a mock login entry point and a dashboard for reviewing balances, recent activity, and navigation between finance sections.
core_goals: Sign in with a mock email and password to access the dashboard
Review overall trip or monthly finance status from the home dashboard
Create monthly or trip funds with target budgets
Record payments or expenses against a selected fund
Delete recorded payments when they are no longer needed
Switch between Home, Expenses, Budget, and Trips sections

features.name: Mock Login
features.description: Enter a valid email and any non-empty password to access the dashboard through the app's mock sign-in flow.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> View the sign-in screen -> Enter a valid email address -> Enter a non-empty password -> Toggle remember-me if desired -> Submit the sign-in form -> See the dashboard home view

features.name: Dashboard Overview
features.description: Review the current finance snapshot, including remaining funds, spending totals, category breakdown, recent expenses, and daily allowance on the home screen.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Sign in with valid credentials -> View the home dashboard -> Review amount remaining -> Review total budget and spent amount -> Review category breakdown -> Review recent expenses -> Review daily allowance
Navigate to / -> Sign in with valid credentials -> View the home dashboard -> Open the expenses view from the View All action -> See the Expenses section

features.name: Fund Creation And Selection
features.description: Create monthly or trip funds with target budgets and choose an existing fund for tracking.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Sign in with valid credentials -> Open the Budget section -> Choose Month -> Enter a fund name -> Enter a target amount -> Create the new month fund -> See the fund appear in the list

Navigate to / -> Sign in with valid credentials -> Open the Budget section -> Choose Trip -> Enter a fund name -> Enter a target amount -> Create the new trip fund -> See the trip fund appear in the list -> Select the created fund for tracking

features.name: Payment Creation
features.description: Add an expense or saved payment to the selected fund and update the displayed totals.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Sign in with valid credentials -> Open the Expenses section -> Review the currently selected fund total -> Enter a payment name -> Enter an amount -> Enter who paid -> Choose a date -> Enter an optional note -> Save the payment -> See the payment appear in the list -> See the saved and spent totals update

features.name: Payment Deletion
features.description: Remove an existing payment from the selected fund and reflect the updated totals.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Sign in with valid credentials -> Open the Expenses section -> Locate an existing payment -> Delete the payment -> See the payment disappear from the list -> See the saved and remaining totals update

features.name: Bottom Navigation
features.description: Switch between the main app sections using the bottom navigation and confirm the visible content changes accordingly.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Sign in with valid credentials -> Select Home from the bottom navigation -> See the home dashboard content and active nav state
Navigate to / -> Sign in with valid credentials -> Select Expenses from the bottom navigation -> See the expenses content and active nav state
Navigate to / -> Sign in with valid credentials -> Select Budget from the bottom navigation -> See the budget content and active nav state
Navigate to / -> Sign in with valid credentials -> Select Trips from the bottom navigation -> See the trips content and active nav state

routes.description: Mock login screen followed by the trip finance dashboard.

features.name: Mock Login
features.description: User enters any valid email and non-empty password to access the dashboard.
user_interactions: View app brand and sign-in form
Confirm dashboard appears after successful mock login

features.name: Dashboard Overview
features.description: User reviews current fund status, budget, spending, category breakdown, recent expenses, and daily allowance.
user_interactions: Review total budget and spent amount
Click View All to open Expenses
Click floating add button to open Expenses

features.name: Fund Creation And Selection
features.description: User creates monthly or trip funds with target budgets and selects funds for tracking.
user_interactions: Click Build new month or Build new trip
Select a created fund from the list

api_calls.endpoint: Firestore collection paymentGroups when Firebase is configured
localStorage key trip-finance-groups when Firebase is not configured

features.name: Payment Creation
features.description: User records an expense or saved payment against the selected fund.
user_interactions: Confirm payment appears in the payment list

api_calls.endpoint: Firestore collection payments when Firebase is configured
localStorage key trip-finance-payments when Firebase is not configured

features.name: Payment Deletion
features.description: User deletes an existing payment and totals update.
user_interactions: Click the delete button for that payment
Confirm saved and remaining totals update

api_calls.endpoint: Firestore document in payments collection when Firebase is configured
localStorage key trip-finance-payments when Firebase is not configured

features.name: Bottom Navigation
features.description: User switches between Home, Expenses, Budget, and Trips sections.
user_interactions: Confirm active nav state and visible content change

known_limitations.issue: Login is mock local state only and does not validate against a backend.
known_limitations.impact: Any non-empty credentials sign in; security/authentication tests should treat this as an intentional demo behavior.

known_limitations.issue: The app has no route-level URLs for individual tabs.
known_limitations.impact: Test navigation must use visible UI controls rather than direct deep links.

known_limitations.issue: Firebase is optional and absent environment values fall back to localStorage.
known_limitations.impact: Cloud database behavior is not exercised unless real Firebase environment variables are configured.
